summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #682 in fogodev/ars-ui by fixing edge cases in progress/meter components and getting the PR to a clean second-pass review state.
summary.outcome: Two fix commits were pushed (7938bea8 and a follow-up), all five original Codex threads were replied to and resolved, a second Codex review pass was triggered and completed with four additional edge cases found and addressed, and all CI checks passed.

repo: Progress component uses a Bindable abstraction that must be reconstructed when value ownership switches between controlled and uncontrolled modes.
Progress percent math requires normalization for invalid ranges and must return 0 for non-finite values.
Meter value attributes must sanitize non-finite values before serializing ARIA/native attrs.
aria-valuenow for progress must be clamped to the exposed min/max range; meter aria-valuenow and native value must use the clamped sanitized value.
Completion state must not be derived from malformed raw bounds; controlled progress events must not mutate value before prop sync.
The repo uses cargo llvm-cov for coverage, proptest for state machine tests (run with --ignored flag), and has per-crate feature flags like i18n.

code.finding: Progress bounds normalization and percent calculation logic; non-finite values must short-circuit to 0.
Controlled/uncontrolled prop sync and Bindable reconstruction logic lives here.
aria-valuenow clamping to exposed min/max range.
Meter sanitizes non-finite values before setting ARIA and native value attributes; aria-valuenow and native value use the clamped sanitized value.
Integration tests for meter/stat/progress edge cases including invalid bounds, non-finite values, and controlled update semantics.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via gh pr comment.
Codex review thread resolution requires both a reply via REST (gh api -X POST .../comments -F in_reply_to=...) and a separate GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation per thread ID.
Polling CI and review state was done with sleep+gh loops; reactions on the issue (👀) indicate Codex is actively reviewing.

Test suite for data_display components is split: integration tests (-p ars-components --test data_display_meter_stat_progress), lib unit tests (--lib with filter), and proptest state machines (--ignored flag required).
Full local validation sequence before push: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo llvm-cov coverage summary, proptest state machines.

friction: Codex second pass surfaced four additional edge cases after the first fix commit, requiring a second round of patches, tests, and CI wait cycles.
CI polling required multiple 120-second sleep loops to track progress through formatting, clippy, workspace check, and unit test stages sequentially.
Resolving Codex threads required two separate API calls per thread (REST reply + GraphQL resolve mutation) with different ID formats (database integer vs. node ID).
open_items: Second Codex review pass result not confirmed in transcript — unclear if the follow-up commit received a clean approval or will trigger further review threads.
Coverage numbers from cargo llvm-cov were checked as summary-only; no explicit coverage threshold enforcement or tracking noted.
